evidence: The Company continues to focus on leveraging digital technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI) and advanced analytics across its operations.
AI systems require significant investments in data infrastructure, high quality data inputs, skilled personnel, cybersecurity safeguards, and ongoing monitoring.
The complexity of AI systems demands continuous investment, which could strain resources and impact other areas of the business.
The Company continues to focus on leveraging digital technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI) and advanced analytics across its operations.
The Company's ability to realize the anticipated benefits of AI initiatives depends on a number of factors, many of which are outside of its control.
AI systems may fail to perform as expected under certain conditions or become vulnerable to adversarial attacks that manipulate the AI's output.
Compliance with these emerging and changing regulations may require ADM to incur substantial costs and make changes to its business practices and complex adjustments to its AI systems.
Legal challenges related to intellectual property, liability for AI-driven decisions, and potential misuse of AI present significant risks.

limitations: No quantified AI investment amounts or program-level budget figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity scored on named commitment language only.
AI narrative is largely risk-disclosure framing with no named deployed use cases or production metrics; concreteness capped at 2.

Risk section material is present and scores well; governance detail (CISO, Council, Board reporting) supports score 4 but no quantified risk metrics or formal AI-specific oversight body distinct from cybersecurity governance is named, preventing score 5.
